## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about joint venture webinars. A little bit of definition here for a moment. Joint venture, we commonly call that JV. Joint venture webinar is where someone else sends traffic to a webinar that you run. Generally, this is somebody who has a list, an email list of people who are in your target avatar, and you deal with them, where they're going to send some emails or promotions to the people on their list saying, hey, you should come watch this webinar that I'm going to hold with Don on such and such a day at such and such a time. And they're sending that traffic to a special URL that tracks the traffic as it comes from them and actually assigns that person to them. So then you know exactly which people came from them in your tracking process. Then you run the webinar and pay them a commission on the cells that you make to those people that they send you. Right, so that's the general theory. They're sending you traffic using a special link that tracks that person to them. If that person buys, you send them a percentage of that cell. And I want to make it clear here that when I'm referring to joint venture webinars, I'm specifically referring here to joint venture webinars with just one partner at a time. Now I realize you can do multiple partners on one webinar, but it's not nearly as effective. So what I'm recommending that you do is one partner at a time. And by doing this, I find that your conversion rate goes way higher when you do them just one at a time. Because what you end up doing is you have that joint venture partner actually hosting the webinar, personalizing it to his or her audience, and then offering a bonus for purchase of your product. So they're saying, hey, I brought Don on to talk to you because I felt like the message that Don has bring in you is perfect for you. And I'm as a person who is helping you to achieve whatever your goal is, then Don is a perfect person to come in and help you do that. And so here he is, here's Don. And then also as part of this, they will occasionally jump in, maybe clarify something for their audience, or maybe say, hey, this supports this thing that I teach over here or whatever it is. And then at the end of it, they'll come on and say, hey, I feel like so important for you to purchase this product that I put together a special bonus just for you if you'll do that. So that's the reason why I recommend that you do them

## [03:02 - 06:03]

one at a time. And this video and the other video where we talk about joint venture webinar bonuses, they're referring to one at a time, working with one joint venture partner at a time. So JV webinars are great. There's a lot of reasons why I really like them in this entire webinar funnel formula model. The first thing is that these can happen at any time, whether you're in launch mode or not. So let's say that you do launch to your list, the classic product launch formula, Jeff Walker style launch, and you do that in September. Well, you could run a JV webinar to Sandra's list in April. And it can be a total standalone, has nothing to do, your list doesn't even know that that is happening. She's sending emails out to her list, sending them into your webinar. And so this enables you to be able to basically make money any time of year, not just from a product that you would normally only launch once during that year. It also can happen without your needing mail, your list. And so your list is not even aware of it. And as I said, it can bring in this income consistently throughout the year, as well as enabling you to be introduced to your JV partners list and also to that JV partner. So that they then will want to promote you and your product on your next launch. So let's go back to that analogy I used before with Sandra. So you run a JV webinar to her list in April, and then you go back to her and you say, hey, Sandra, that works so well. I'm doing my major launch in September. How'd you like to promote that to your list in September? It's six months from now. And so a lot of people who didn't buy now will be ready to buy then. And there will be others who will be new to your list by then as well as those who were with your list earlier may now be interested because their life or their business has changed since that time. So I already made you a lot of money doing this webinar. Why don't you promote my launch again in six months? In other words, this is a great JV recruitment tool for your Jeff Walker style product launch formula launches. They're also great because they can significantly build your list and your impact in the world. Because as Sandra sends that mailing out to her people, those people are getting added to your email list. ## [09:04 - 12:04]

Let me tell you a brief example of this. I one time got a call from somebody who I knew of in the industry. I knew that they had a big list. I knew that they had a good product and that they were pretty good at selling it. And so they asked me to sponsor to do a JV webinar to my list. And so I ended up, I was in a hurry. I can't remember exactly what the reason was. Normally I asked to watch a recording of a previous webinar before I'll agree to promote it to my list. And I listened to that webinar to make sure that they actually add value, they teach something. So even if people don't buy, they feel like they got something out of it. Well, here's what happened. I didn't do that research. And this person came on and their webinar was one of those that I talked about earlier in this program where I said there is a group of people who teach a content free webinar. The whole webinar is a sales pitch. There is nothing worth writing down. There is nothing worth recording. The whole thing is hype. And look at all the money you can make and all that kind of stuff. And that's what this person's webinar was. And I kept interrupting him and trying to get him to give content. And you could tell from his tone of voice, he's getting madder and madder and madder at me because I am destroying his flow of his webinar. And yet at the same time, I'm watching my people's comments come in saying, this is worthless. This is a total waste of my time. Goodbye, I'm leaving. And I'm realizing that I have risked my relationship with my list to promote his webinar and he was destroying that relationship with my people. And so that's one of the principles of this relationship business piece is you're asking someone to risk their relationship with their list in order to be able to support you. And so first off, please, please, please, don't do content free webinars. I hope you've gotten that message through this particular program. But secondly, also recognize that as you build this relationship with this JVs, they don't know that yet. ## [15:07 - 18:09]

who are freelancers buy besides training on how to be better freelancers? Another group of people is people who have blogs that your target avatar reads, or have podcasts that they listen to, or have social media followings that might be interested. This is actually becoming much more of a factor now where there are people who are building these huge lists on Instagram and Facebook and WhatsApp and things like this, people who are in a target audience, they may never have actually built a list, but they have a large group of people that they can influence just through social media. Don't discount that group of people also. Another side strategy is what I call the leaderboard strategy. Basically, you go out and you find somebody who's doing a launch to your target avatar. You sign up to promote their product and then you look for their leaderboard emails. So those of you who don't know what a leaderboard email is, one of the things that you do in a product launch formula style launch, when you get a bunch of joint ventures, partners who are all promoting your product, you send out emails to them that are basically using social proof to say, hey, you ought to be promoting this too. And one of those things that you oftentimes do is you'll do, let's say, it's day one of cart open. So it's the product's now for sale. And so you'll put this leaderboard up. The number one seller is this person, the number two is this. And so by putting that leaderboard up, then people who are the number three spot may say, well, I think I can get to number one. And all I had to do is beat these two people. So they'll go off and they'll actually promote harder to try to get to the top of that list. It's a competitive thing. And the beauty of that is that when you go and you see those emails, you then are seeing the list of people who are willing to promote things as a JV partner and who are good at it. Because they gotta be good at it to be at the top of these lists. And so that's a great place to go and find a list of names of people who are good at promoting

to your particular target audience. Now, while we're on the subject, there's no faster way to get someone's attention than to get yourself onto their leaderboard.
